items.title: Can We Explore Transcendence Via Art and Mind? | Episode 2501 | Closer To Truth
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>What is the three-way relationship among mind, art, and transcendence? What is it about art that can elicit the sense of transcendence, going beyond the self, bursting the constraining bonds of semantic language? What is it about transcendence that it can be expressed by art? What in fact is transcendence, from a momentary escape from self to a full- blown mystical experience? How does the art-transcendence nexus engage the mind? We describe key terms and ideas.

Featuring interviews with Simone Schnall, Julio Bermudez, Judith Wolfe, Oludamini Ogunnaike, Jonathan Berger, and Anjan Chatterjee.

Make a donation to Closer To Truth to help us continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

Closer To Truth, hosted by Robert Lawrence Kuhn and directed by Peter Getzels, presents the world’s greatest thinkers exploring humanity’s deepest questions. Discover fundamental issues of existence. Engage new and diverse ways of thinking. Appreciate intense debates. Share your own opinions. Seek your own answers.</article>

items.title: Robin Hanson on AI and Large Language Models | Closer To Truth Chats
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Robots may one day rule the world, but what is a robot-ruled Earth like? Dr. Robin Hanson explores the implications of a future world in which researchers have not created artificial general intelligence but have learned to copy humans onto computers, creating "ems," or emulated people, who quickly come to outnumber the real ones.

Robin Hanson is an Associate Professor of Economics, and received his Ph.D in social sciences from Caltech. He joined George Mason's economics faculty in 1999 after completing a two-year post-doc at U.C Berkely. His major fields of interest include health policy, regulation, and formal political theory.

Watch more CTT Chats here: https://t.ly/jJI7e

This Chat was made possible by MindFest 2025: "Sentience, Autonomy, and the Future of Human-AI Interaction", a two-day event presented by the Center for the Future Mind.

To learn more about groundbreaking innovation in AI, neuroscience, and the study of consciousness, visit the Center for the Future Mind, view MindFest videos, and subscribe to their newsletter, visit: https://shorturl.at/mYtqz.</article>

items.title: Galen Strawson - Are There Too Many Theories of Consciousness?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Follow us on Instagram for daily videos, updates, and announcements: https://www.instagram.com/closertotruth/

What is the current state of the consciousness studies field? What are the major issues and trends? What kinds of experiments are being conducted? What kinds of arguments are being made?

Make a tax-deductible donation of any amount to help support Closer To Truth continue making content like this: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

Galen John Strawson is a British analytic philosopher and literary critic who works primarily on philosophy of mind, metaphysics, John Locke, David Hume, Immanuel Kant, and Friedrich Nietzsche.

items.title: Alan Torrance - How Does Salvation Work?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you get your podcasts: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

The Christian claim is that salvation is the ultimate goal of human life. But what literally is salvation and how does one obtain it? How to comprehend each element of the salvation process and the way they work together? One need not be a Christian to appreciate the philosophical conundrums.

Follow Closer To Truth on Instagram for updates, announcements, and videos: https://shorturl.at/EzYo9

Alan Torrance is a Scottish theologian, academic and ordained minister of the Church of Scotland. Torrance was ordained as a minister in the Church of Scotland in 1984. His academic career began at the Knox Theological Hall and the University of Otago, Dunedin, New Zealand.

items.title: Liad Mudrik - Theories of Consciousness
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Closer To Truth is now on Bluesky! Follow us for updates, announcements, and more: https://shorturl.at/GSR4k

Explore diverse theories of consciousness including materialism, dualism, panpsychism, idealism and diverse ways of thinking about consciousness from the perspectives of neuroscience, philosophy, wisdom traditions. Compare and contrast diverse theories of consciousness as they are arrayed on a “Landscape of Consciousness”.

Make a donation to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

Professor Liad Mudrik is a researcher at the school of psychological sciences and Sagol school of neuroscience at Tel Aviv University. Her research focuses on conscious experience, its neural mechanisms and functions.

items.title: Stephen Wolfram on Universal Computation: Revolutionizing the World | Closer To Truth Chats
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>How does complexity emerge from simplicity? In this interview, Stephen Wolfram explores the concept of universal computation and how simple rules can generate intricate, seemingly random patterns. This perspective challenges traditional views of complexity in physics, biology, and beyond, revealing deep insights into the computational universe.

From the foundations of cellular automata to the discovery of surprising patterns in nature, Wolfram shows how computation reshapes our understanding of reality and revolutionizes the way we think about science.

Watch more CTT Chats here: https://t.ly/jJI7e

Stephen Wolfram is a British-American computer scientist, physicist, and businessman. He is known for his work in computer algebra and theoretical physics. In 2012, he was named a fellow of the American Mathematical Society.</article>

items.title: Alex Gómez-Marín - Can AI Become Conscious?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Like us on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l

AI consciousness, its possibility or probability, has burst into public debate, eliciting all kinds of issues from AI ethics and rights to AI going rogue and harming humanity. We explore diverse views; we argue that AI consciousness depends on theories of consciousness.

Make a donation of any amount to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions: https://shorturl.at/Xsn2z

Àlex Gómez-Marín is a Spanish physicist turned neuroscientist. He holds a PhD in theoretical physics and a Masters in biophysics from the University of Barcelona. He was a research fellow at the EMBL-CRG Centre for Genomic Regulation and at the Champalimaud Centre for the Unknown in Lisbon. His research spans from the origins of the arrow of time in inert systems, to the neurobiology of action-perception in living organisms such as flies, worms, mice, and humans.

items.title: Timothy O'Connor - Classical Arguments for God?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Contribute what you can to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without paywalls: https://shorturl.at/l3q6G

What are the classical arguments used to prove the existence of God? For the Cosmological Argument we argue First Cause; from the Ontological Argument we argue Necessary Being; from Teleology we argue Design and Purpose. All are refuted and in turn defended in what seems to be a philosophical arms race. In the end, no one believes in God because of arguments, but they do help to develop our thinking about God.

Watch more videos on this topic here: https://shorturl.at/ovqjh

Timothy O'Connor is Professor of Philosophy at Indiana University Bloomington. He has published widely in the areas of metaphysics, philosophy of mind and action, and philosophy of religion.

Wear your support for the show with a Closer To Truth merchandise purchase: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

Denis Noble is a British physiologist and biologist who held the Burdon Sanderson Chair of Cardiovascular Physiology at the University of Oxford from 1984 to 2004 and was appointed professor emeritus and co-director of computational physiology.

items.title: Deepak Chopra - Is Consciousness Ultimate Reality?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Like us on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l

Is consciousness deepest reality, the ground of being of the cosmos? If the question is “What brought all into existence?” the answer is “Consciousness”. Some say this is a ‘cosmic consciousness’ of which our personal consciousness is a small part. Others, that the ultimate consciousness is God. Others, that consciousness and cosmos are both deep reality.

Make a donation of any amount to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions: https://shorturl.at/Xsn2z

Deepak Chopra is an Indian-American author, new age guru, and alternative medicine advocate. A prominent figure in the New Age movement, his books and videos have made him one of the best-known and wealthiest figures in alternative medicine.

items.title: James Hughes on Transhumanism and Personal Identity | Closer To Truth Chats
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>James Hughes Ph.D. is the Executive Director of the Institute for Ethics and Emerging Technologies, and a bioethicist and sociologist who serves as the Associate Provost for Institutional Research, Assessment and Planning for the University of Massachusetts Boston. He holds a doctorate in sociology from the University of Chicago, where he also taught bioethics at the MacLean Center for Clinical Medical Ethics. Dr. Hughes is author of "Citizen Cyborg" and co-editor of "Surviving the Machine Age".

Watch more CTT Chats here: https://t.ly/jJI7e

items.title: Bas van Fraassen - Can Moral Theory Affect Deep Ontology?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Follow us on Instagram for daily videos, updates, and announcements: https://www.instagram.com/closertotruth/

Normally, if we have a belief system, such as a particular religion, some kind of morality follows. Can the direction of causation go in reverse? If we commit to a particular morality, whether absolute or relative, are there implications for ultimate reality? Can morality say anything about the deep nature of the world?

Make a tax-deductible donation of any amount to help support Closer To Truth continue making content like this: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

Bastiaan Cornelis "Bas" van Fraassen is a Dutch-American philosopher noted for his contributions to philosophy of science, epistemology and formal logic. He is a Distinguished Professor of Philosophy at San Francisco State University and the McCosh Professor of Philosophy Emeritus at Princeton University.

Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

items.title: Derek Turner - Evolution and Religion?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you get your podcasts: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

Two kinds of questions describe the relationship between evolution and religion. The first explores discrepancies, even contradictions, between the science of evolution and the beliefs of religion. The second examines how religion itself evolved.

Follow Closer To Truth on Instagram for updates, announcements, and videos: https://shorturl.at/EzYo9

Derek Turner teaches philosophy at Connecticut College where he is also the Karla Heurich Harrison ’28 Director of the Goodwin-Niering Center for the Environment. Turner’s research focuses on philosophical issues in historical science, especially paleontology and the earth sciences.
